Operation 'getNextAvailableIp' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current summary and description are identical and lack specificity. The description needs to clarify: (1) what input is required (subnet ID), (2) what the operation returns (an available IP address), (3) important side effect clarification that this does NOT allocate/reserve the IP (only identifies it), and (4) use case context. These additions improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation, and prevent misunderstanding about whether the IP is actually reserved.

data.current_summary: Get next available IP in subnet
data.current_description: Get next available IP in subnet
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve next available IP address from subnet
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the next available IP address within a specified subnet. Accepts a subnet ID as input and returns an available IP address that has not been allocated to any device or resource. This operation does not reserve or allocate the IP address - it only identifies the next available address. Useful for determining which IP to assign before creating a new device or network resource.
data.reasoning: The current summary and description are identical and lack specificity. The description needs to clarify: (1) what input is required (subnet ID), (2) what the operation returns (an available IP address), (3) important side effect clarification that this does NOT allocate/reserve the IP (only identifies it), and (4) use case context. These additions improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation, and prevent misunderstanding about whether the IP is actually reserved.
